markers.evidence_type: transgelin, defining marker for inflammatory/contractile pericyte subtype
smooth muscle actin, high expression in inflammatory pericytes
MCP-1, inflammatory chemokine upregulated in activated pericytes
adhesion molecule upregulated in inflammatory pericytes

other_findings.finding: TAGLN+ inflammatory pericytes represent the activated pericyte subpopulation in human skin with upregulated expression of inflammatory cytokines, chemokines, and adhesion molecules compared to RGS5+ non-inflammatory pericytes
Two major pericyte populations are present in human skin: RGS5+ (non-inflammatory/Pericyte_1) and TAGLN+ (inflammatory/contractile/Pericyte_2)
Inflammatory pericytes can regulate vascular permeability and leukocyte trafficking into skin tissue during inflammation

evidence_quotes.quote: Regarding perivascular cell populations, we found three populations of pericytes... there were two major pericyte populations, which we denoted RGS5+ and TAGLN+ based on high expression of those genes
evidence_quotes.source_file: Ganier et al. (2024) Multiscale spatial mapping of cell populations across the skin layers

evidence_quotes.quote: Pericytes are a specialised mesenchymal cell population embedded in the basement membrane of capillaries... identified and distinguished from perivascular adventitial fibroblasts by high expression of ACTA2, RGS5, and PDGFRB
evidence_quotes.source_file: Ganier et al. (2024) Multiscale spatial mapping of cell populations across the skin layers
